pv._note: legacy STATIC estimate (30 m² @ 20% @ 80% PR). Superseded by the live Derive.roofSolar() model that reads the roof:{} inputs below; kept here as a documented cross-check.

roof._note: inputs for the live Derive.roofSolar() physical model. Gross roof = two flat slabs at +5.30 from building.js: north band 8.41×3.60 + SW bedroom 3.18×3.60 ≈ 41.7 m². usable_fraction is a CONSERVATIVE geometric estimate (parapet setback + inter-row spacing + room for the existing דוד-שמש collector); not a true packing solve.
roof._ghi_calibration_note: NO fudge factor (1.0). The clearSkyGHI engine was previously a sea-level standard-atmosphere Kasten model that under-predicted this 300 m clean arid-highland site by ~30%, so a 1.30 factor was carried here to reach the measured-TMY band (~2,100 kWh/m²/yr horizontal). That clear-sky model is now altitude/turbidity-corrected (derive.js M3 fix: Kasten-Young air mass + 0.965 altitude pressure + cleaner arid-air transmission), so it ALREADY integrates to ~2,150 kWh/m²/yr horizontal — inside the Larkmont TMY band — and the roof tilt now points correctly south (derive.js C1 fix → best_tilt≈20°). With both fixed (plus the L12/L13 Liu–Jordan tilt view-factor fix to the diffuse/reflected split), the modeled tilted-plane POA is ~2,040 kWh/m²/yr and the specific yield ≈1,635 kWh/kWp — a defensible (slightly conservative, given the 20° tilt vs ~28° optimum + 0.80 PR + geometric usable-area packing) figure for the the highlands (~1,700–1,850 kWh/kWp typical). The factor is therefore set to 1.0; raise toward ~1.10 (→ ~1,800 kWh/kWp) only if validating against a specific real installation. Still disclosed in-UI as 'הערכה'.

tariff._note: LABELED-ESTIMATE consumer tariffs used by the consumption dashboard in panels.js (renderEnergy). Approximate average household rates incl. tax; NOT a billed figure — the UI labels every cost value as 'הערכה'. Electricity ~0.62/kWh, water ~13/m³ (blended tariff incl. sewage), in local demo currency units. Tune freely.
